products.title: All-In-One Planted Aquarium Kit - Cube
products.body_html:
The Glass Aqua All-In-One Planted Aquarium Tank Kit is a complete aquascaping kit that includes everything needed to create a freshwater aquascape layout. A perfect canvas, our all-in-one planted aquarium kit includes the aquarium tank, aquarium filter, LED light, CO2, aquarium substrate soil, hardscape, and tweezers. All that's left is choosing your aquatic plants to complete it! Each item included is curated by our team to ensure that our aquascaping kit is aesthetic and cost-effective while still providing quality essentials needed to start a successful planted aquarium tank. Our all-in-one planted aquarium kit is suitable for any occasion and all skill levels. Whether you're a beginner who would like some guidance or an advance planted tank enthusiast looking for a solid setup for your next aquarium project.

Aquarium Kit Includes:
UNS Rimless 30A All-In-One Cube Glass Aquarium Tank (approximately 4.9 gallons) with black leveling mat
Chihiros Nano LED Light 
Aquario NEO CO2 DIY Bottle Kit
3L bag of UNS Controsoil Substrate
5LBS of random rocks or 3 pieces of Spider Wood Nano
UNS aquascaping tweezers

Aquarium Kit Breakdown:

The aquarium tank included is a beautiful 30A rimless glass aquarium by Ultum Nature Systems. This special edition aquarium tank features a sleek, built-in filter eliminating the research prompted when finding the correct aquarium filter. The filtration chamber includes a coarse aquarium sponge, quality porous ceramic bio-media and a reliable Dymax water pump to provide consistent flow to the entire aquarium tank.

Quality lighting is essential when promoting aquatic plant growth. Densely planted aquarium tanks are a safe haven for freshwater fish and shrimp species. The Flat One Nano LED light by ONF is a beautiful aquarium LED lighting solution that is constructed from simple materials while remaining extremely practical for maintenance and is resistant to unsightly rust buildup. With 3 lighting levels that administer a slow ramp up and down, this aquarium LED will illuminate an aquarium tank beautifully while meeting the needs of different aquatic plant light requirements and ensuring your aquarium tank inhabitants aren't spooked and adjust well to their new environment.

Quality aquarium lighting and CO2 injection go hand in hand. Included in this aquascaping kit is the NEO CO2 Kit by Aquario. The introduction of CO2 in a planted aquarium tank allows aquatic plants to thrive and grow more efficiently, while also giving them the ability to out-compete algae for nutrients in the water column. The NEO CO2 Kit by Aquario contains simple setup instructions along with refill media to ease beginners into CO2 injection. This unit is easy to move and can be swapped out or upgraded to preference!


Planted aquarium substrate soil provides a source of crucial nutrients for aquatic plants. Controsoil by Ultum Nature Systems is ready to use out of the bag and does not require any rinsing. Controsoil doesn't leech excessive nutrients reducing the potential for algae growth and buffers water to a slightly acidic pH, providing a well-rounded environment for aquarium plants and tank inhabitants.


Aquascaping hardscape material acts as building blocks. Our complete aquascaping kit contains some of the most popular types of hardscape material. Seiryu Stone is a classic stone type used commonly in the planted tank hobby for creating Iwagumi layouts or as a base for aquarium driftwood. Pacific wood features beautiful knots and character. Aquarium wood is known for its buoyancy and should always be boiled or soaked thoroughly before use to ensure the pieces are waterlogged! Pacific Wood is one of the densest woods and does not take as long to become waterlogged, making it perfect for those who are short on time but love the natural look aquarium wood provides. Tools complete the kit, allowing for precision planting to ensure aquatic plants grow in lush and full!

products.title: Hydrocotyle Tripartita Mini Variegated
products.body_html:
Hydrocotyle tripartita ‘Mini’ Variegated is a variation of the classic form, featuring the same clover-shaped leaves accented with subtle cream and white tones along the edges. This added contrast brings a lighter, more delicate look while maintaining a similar growth habit to the original.

Often used in the midground, this plant spreads through runners, forming bushy clusters with occasional trailing vines as it continues to grow. The variegated foliage stands out against darker green plants and contrasts beautifully with stone or driftwood!

Hydrocotyle tripartita ‘Mini’ Variegated is slightly more sensitive than the standard form due to reduced chlorophyll, making stable conditions more important for long-term health. Provide medium to high lighting, CO₂ injection, and regular fertilization. With proper care, it will grow slowly and steadily. In lower light conditions, growth may become sparse or the plant may revert to its original coloration. Propagation is simple. Cut along the stem and replant into the substrate to encourage fuller coverage.

Aquascaping Uses
- Foreground to midground transitional plant
- Midground accent plant for nature-style aquascaping
- Filling in negative space in planted aquariums
- Suitable as a vining plant in terrarium or paludarium tanks

Common Name Tripartita mini variegated
Scientific Name Hydrocotyle tripartita ‘mini’
Difficulty Level Intermediate
Lighting Level Medium to High
CO2 Requirement Recommended
Growth Type Stem
Aquascape Placement Midground
Propagation Method Trim and replant
Growth Rate Moderate to Fast
Size 3-7”+

products.title: 2HR Aquarist APT Fix
products.body_html:
APT Fix by 2HR Aquarist is a targeted algae treatment for planted aquariums! And from our experience, it’s one of the most effective (but gentle) solutions when you’re dealing with an algae bloom that’s grown beyond manual removal or natural control methods.

Its fast-acting formula works directly on affected areas and biodegrades completely after treatment. APT Fix is especially effective against dreaded Black Beard Algae (BBA), BGA, Staghorn, and filmanentous types like green hair, fuzz, and string algae.

Each bottle includes a spot-dosing pipette for precise and easy application to affected areas. When used as directed, APT Fix is safe for most freshwater fish and shrimp.

APT Fix in action in our UNS 45U Iwagumi planted aquarium, showing targeted treatment of problem algae:

APT Fix Highlights
- Copper-free! Safe for fish and shrimp when used as directed
- Fast-acting treatment for common aquarium algae
- Gently biodegrades and will not disrupt aquarium cycle

Size: 300ml / 10.14FL Oz.
Dosing Instructions: Spot dose affected areas. Use up to 1ml per 2.5 Gallons

TIP! Start with half the recommended dosage for 3–7 days before increasing if needed. When spot dosing plants, temporarily turn off filtration to stop flow. For hardscape treatment, partially drain the aquarium to expose affected areas before applying. After treatment, perform a water change to help maintain balance.

Ingredients: 3% Epoxy Aldehydes

products.title: 2HR Aquarist APT 1
products.body_html:
APT 1 by 2HR Aquarist is an all-in-one aquarium plant fertilizer designed for new planted tanks or setups with lighter plant mass. Its balanced formula excludes nitrate and phosphate, helping reduce excess nutrients in the early stages when most freshly planted aquascapes simply don’t need them yet.

If you’re looking for a no-fuss, easy-to-dose liquid fertilizer, APT 1 is one of our go-to choices. It provides comprehensive nutrition while keeping things simple, supporting healthy plant growth without overloading the system. Each bottle includes a calibrated pump and straightforward instructions, making it easy to stay consistent. One bottle is estimated to last approximately 3 months based on a standard 30-gallon planted tank.

Choosing the Right APT Fertilizer
APT 1 is designed for newly planted tanks or aquariums with lower plant mass and nutrient demand. As plants mature and nutrient requirements grow, APT 3 provide a more complete solutions with added nitrate and phosphate to support continued growth and demand.

APT 1 Zero Highlights
- Ideal for new aquascapes, Iwagumi layouts or lightly planted tanks, and aquariums that contain more fish and fewer plants
- Nitrate and phosphate-free formula
- Easy daily dosing with included calibrated pump
- Looking for a stronger formula? Upgrade to APT 3!

Size: 300ml / 10.14FL Oz.
Dosing Instructions: Add 3 pumps per 26 gallons of water daily. Perform a 30-50% weekly water change.

TIP! If you are starting off with a fresh aquascape or have a densely planted setup, start with APT 1 during the first 1-3 months. Monitor growth over time and when plants are mature and nutrient requirements increase, transition to APT 3 to support continued growth!

Guaranteed Analysis

Soluble Potash (K2O) 5.5%
Magnesium (Mg) 0.85%
Iron (Fe) 0.1%

0.1% Chelated Iron
Derived from: Potassium Sulphate, Potassium Chloride, Magnesium Sulphate, DTPA Iron, EDTA Iron, EDDHA Iron

products.title: 2HR Aquarist APT 3
products.body_html:
APT 3 by 2HR Aquarist is a comprehensive, all-in-one aquarium plant fertilizer designed for established planted tanks with high nutrient needs. Its complete formula is packed with macro nutrients (NPK), iron, magnesium, and trace elements to fuel growth and boost color for even the hobby’s most demanding plants. With APT 3, grow the temperamental Rotalas, Ludwigias, and other rare plants often considered advanced in the aquascaping community.

If you have a heavily planted tank, Dutch-style aquascape, or a setup with hungry plants, APT 3 is a complete solution for achieving rich colors and strong growth without significantly increasing maintenance. Each bottle includes a calibrated pump and simple dosing, making it easy to stay on top of your aquarium’s nutrition. One bottle is estimated to last approximately 3 months based on a standard 30-gallon planted tank.

Choosing the Right APT Fertilizer
APT 3 is designed for established aquariums with dense plant growth, nutrient-demanding species, or higher lighting and CO2 injection. For newly planted tanks or setups with lighter plant mass, APT 1 offers a gentler formula for steady plant growth.

APT 3 Complete Highlights
- Ideal for heavily planted setups
- Complete formula with macro (NPK), iron, magnesium, and trace elements

products.title: Staurogyne Repens Variegated
products.body_html:
Staurogyne repens “Variegated” is a variation of the classic S. repens that features bright green leaves marbled with cream and white hues. Its compact growth under higher lighting and signature foliage add contrast and bushy texture to midground areas of a planted aquarium.

Like the standard form, this aquatic plant grows low and gradually forms dense groups when regularly trimmed and replanted. With good lighting and stable water conditions, S. repens is a fantastic choice for creating a natural transition between carpeting plants like Monte Carlo or dwarf hairgrass and taller background plants in planted aquariums.

While generally considered an approachable plant to grow, variegated forms tend to develop more slowly due to reduced chlorophyll. Start with medium lighting levels, dose a complete aquarium plant fertilizer, and inject CO2 to encourage healthier, more compact growth. In setups where lighting is too low, the plant may become leggy, indicating that light levels should be increased.

Aquascaping Uses:
- Midground aquarium plant in nature-style aquascapes
- Transitioning zones between carpet plants and background areas
- Bushy groups along stone or driftwood

Common Name S. Repens
Scientific Name Staurogyne repens
Difficulty Level Intermediate
Lighting Level Medium to High
CO2 Requirement Recommended

Aquascape Placement Midground

Used together with UNS Controbond liquid aquarium glue, it creates a strong hold that keeps your aquascape stable through filling, planting, and routine tank maintenance. Controlock acts as the base bonding material while Controbond activates and cures the connection, creating a strong and stable hold between hardscape pieces. An essential aquascaping accessory for scaping dynamic planted tanks.

Each order of Controlock includes one container of bonding material and one bottle of Controbond liquid adhesive to get started. Additional Controbond adhesive is sold separately for refills.

Size: 77g material + 20g liquid adhesive
Instructions: Pinch and pack Controlock between connection points, add Controbond, and allow it to fully cure. A small smoking reaction is normal and indicates the process is working. For full instructions and examples, see below.

- Available in four colors to blend naturally with a variety of popular layout materials.
- Safe for aquarium fish and shrimp once fully cured; avoid contact with skin and eyes.
- Best for bonding rock structures or securing driftwood pieces in place. No more wiggling!

products.title: Centrolepis Drummondiana (Blood Vomit)
products.body_html:
Centrolepis drummondiana, commonly known as Blood Vomit, is a rare aquarium plant that is easy to recognize thanks to its subtle two-tone coloration. Native to Australia, this species has earned many names in the hobby, including Eriocaulon blood vomit and Trithuria species. Its unusual common name is certainly unique, but remains the most widely recognized trade name within the aquarium hobby.

It is a true foreground plant that grows in tight rosettes. When fully submerged, it produces short, vibrant green leaves with a signature deep red base. Unlike many carpeting plants, it does not spread via runners. This quality allows for very controlled, intentional placement in aquascapes. Its root system can become extensive, often growing longer than the leaves themselves, making substrate choice especially important for long-term health. Blood Vomit is considered an advanced aquatic plant. Successful growth requires a quality aquasoil, high lighting, consistent CO2 injection, and soft water conditions. Pigmentation can be improved with a complete liquid plant fertilizer.

Because of its slow growth, maintaining water quality is important to prevent algae from growing on its leaves. It won’t propagate on its own, but mature rosettes can be gently split into smaller sections and replanted. When grown successfully, Blood Vomit is a highly rewarding aquatic plant that adds beautiful detail and unique character to a planted aquarium.

Common Name Blood Vomit
Care Advanced
Lighting High
CO2 Rquired
Aquascape Placement Foreground
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Slow
Size <2”

products.title: Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa
products.body_html:
Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa, also known as Roundfruit Primrose Willow, is a rare form that isn't commonly seen in aquascaping. Native to wetlands of North America, this aquatic stem plant is perfect for high tech planted aquariums and aquascapers who enjoy growing challenging aquatic plants. It features a unique growth pattern that is compact and almost rosette-like, and spreads horizontally before growing upwards.

When grown in a planted tank with strong lighting, CO2 and a regular dosing routine, Sphaerocarpa develops a vivid color palette that includes orange, brown and rich red tones. Its intense color will surely stand out in any aquascape. It is on the slower side of growth, but patience will be generously rewarded! Provide it with nutrient-rich aquarium substrate and make sure to trim it regularly to encourage new growth and branching behavior. If you're looking for a unique aquarium plant that isn't commonly seen, try this one out.

Common Name Roundfruit Primrose Willow / L. Pilosa
Care Moderate to Advanced
Lighting High
CO2 Recommended
Suggested Placement Midground or Background
Propagation Trim and plant stems
Growth Rate Moderate to Fast
Height 5-12"

featured_image.alt: Ludwigia Sphaerocarpa - Glass Aqua

products.title: Quick Scape 170

products.title: Pellionia Repens Daveauana
products.body_html:
Pellionia repens, commonly known as Watermelon Begonia, is a beautiful and versatile plant well suited for terrariums, vivariums, and enclosed container gardens. Often called the Watermelon Begonia Vine, it’s loved for its asymmetrical leaves and otherworldly color tones that are common with true Begonia species.

This plant is very easygoing and adaptable. Pellionia repens will happily shingle, trail, climb, and vine when given the space, making it an excellent choice for both foreground and background coverage in a terrarium. It thrives in warm environments with high humidity, and its foliage can subtly change depending on the conditions it’s grown in. Because of its forgiving nature and flexible growth habit, it’s a top contender for beginners and a dependable favorite for experienced terrarium and vivarium hobbyists alike.

Common Name Watermelon Begonia
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Moderate
CO2 N/A
Terrarium Placement Attached to hardscape, trailing
Propagation Trim and replant
Growth Rate Moderate
Size Varies

products.title: Eriocaulon Feather Duster
products.body_html:
Eriocaulon sp. “Feather Duster” is a rare, easy-to-grow Eriocaulon species with fine, grass-like leaves that are perfect for the background of a planted aquarium. Similar to popular choices like Vallisneria nana, Eleocharis vivipara, and Cyperus helferi, Feather Duster provides flowing texture with clean, controlled growth that’s easier to maintain.

Despite the picky reputation of the genus, Feather Duster is surprisingly undemanding. Its clumping growth makes it easy to divide and propagate, and moderate lighting is usually sufficient. Stable water quality is key—Feather Duster prefers softer waters, and nutrients can be supplied either through the substrate or with a quality aquatic plant fertilizer.

For stronger, faster growth, increase light levels and plant it in a tank with CO2 injection. It can easily reach the water’s surface, where you can let it swoop naturally or trim as needed, since it responds well to cutting. Overall, its form, soft movement, and lower-maintenance requirements make Eriocaulon Feather Duster one of our top recommendations for aquascapers who want a grass-like background plant.

Common Name Erio Feather Duster
Care Easy to Moderate
Lighting Moderate to High
CO2 Recommended
Aquascape Placement Background
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Moderate
Size 10-15”+

products.title: Tree Moss
products.body_html:
Tree Moss (Climacium dendroides) is an ornamental live moss that resembles tiny pine trees. Perfect for forest-inspired enclosures, it features a tree-like form with feathery, deep-green fronds. As it matures, it can expand into dense colonies, creating a neat woodland effect in terrariums, moss collections, or paludariums.

This variety needs high humidity with consistent moisture. It will tolerate low-light conditions, making it a great choice for low-maintenance setups. In the wild, Tree Moss is often found growing on natural wood in consistently damp environments. If you’re looking for a unique moss for your planted setup, Tree Moss is sure to add unique character and texture.

Common Name Tree Moss
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Moderate
CO2 N/A
Terrarium Placement Attached to wood
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Slow to Moderate
Size 1-3”

products.title: Umbrella Moss (Rhodobryum Giganteum)
products.body_html:
Rhodobryum giganteum, commonly known as Umbrella Moss or Giant Rose Moss, is a unique moss species that produces flower-like growth. When happy and healthy, it forms dense clusters of tight rosettes that resemble tiny blooming umbrellas, adding sculptural texture and visual interest to terrariums and planted displays.

In the wild, Umbrella Moss is typically found growing along the base of trees or on shaded rocks where airflow and stable moisture levels are present. Its growth spreads beneath the surface, with new rosettes emerging above the substrate over time. This live moss performs best in well-ventilated terrariums and similar setups. Plant it in sphagnum or a moisture-retentive substrate without waterlogging and place it in an area that receives filtered light.

If you’re looking for a unique moss for a terrarium, vivarium, or paludarium, Rhodobryum giganteum offers an ideal balance of easy care and uncommon aesthetic.

Approximate size: less than 1"
Each purchase includes 2-4 individual "heads" of live moss. Photo is enlarged to show texture and look of the plant.

products.title: Mini Taiwan Moss
products.body_html:
Mini Taiwan Moss (Taxiphyllum alternans) is a rarer and beautiful aquarium moss with a delicate texture and soft green color. Similar to Weeping Moss, it features horizontal, overhanging growth and adds natural texture and visual interest to any planted aquarium.

Its compact size is perfectly scaled for all tank sizes, especially nano aquariums and smaller aquascapes. Like other mosses, care is relatively simple. It adapts well to low-tech tank settings and can grow nicely in shaded areas of higher-light aquascapes.

Use aquatic moss to cover unnatural gaps in a planted tank layout or attach it to hardscape materials. Incorporating moss adds character, softens hard edges, and gives instant maturity to freshly planted tanks.

Provide Mini Taiwan Moss with clean, stable water parameters and gentle flow as a baseline. CO2 injection will help it grow fuller and form the dense, fluffy tufts seen in popular aquascapes. Propagation is easy—simply separate a portion and reattach or reposition as desired. If you’re trimming moss, we recommend turning off the filter first to prevent unwanted spread and easier clean up!

products.title: Build Your Own Terrarium Plant Pack
products.body_html:
Build Your Own Terrarium Plant Pack

A terrarium is a self-contained garden of live plants inside of a glass vessel. In our case, we like to use rimless tanks and starting one is simple. If you’re a planted tank hobbyist, think of it as an aquarium without the water changes. Terrariums feature humid, tropical environments, making them perfect for a mixture of terrestrial and aquatic plant species. Many aquarium plants adapt beautifully to emersed growth (out of water), and when paired with terrestrials, they create a familiar yet refreshingly different style of plants display with lower maintenance needs.

Terrariums generally require less equipment, removing the guesswork of selecting filters, CO2 systems, or complex hardware. This customizable plant pack features a curated mix of aquatic, terrestrial, and tropical jungle plant species that will shine in terrarium environments.

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Jade
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Brownie Jade is one of many buce varieties. Like others, it is a resilient, slow-growing plant that is often dubbed a great beginner plant for its hardiness. While this is true, Bucephalandra have so much potential when provided with consistent care and stability in planted aquariums.

To grow happy and healthy buce, go back to the basics. Tanks should be mature with fully established cycles to prevent melt and alleviate stress on the plant during transition periods. Plant your Buce in a spot that receives low to moderate lighting and good, indirect flow. CO2 injection and light fertilization can help improve form and color, but tank cleanliness is key! Overall, Bucephalandra is a genus loved by most. These aquatic rhizomes can grow in a variety of settings and observing the transition from emersed foliage to fully submerged growth is rewarding. Colors morph and many species (not all) feature the signature iridescent shimmer when placed under specific aquarium lights. Make sure to trim away old or dead leaves and keep up on tank maintenance!

Common Name Buce Brownie Jade
Care Easy
Lighting Moderate
CO2 Recommended
Aquascape Placement Attached to hardscape
Propagation Separate rhizomes
Growth Rate Moderate
Size Varies

products.title: Echinodorus Red Phoenix
products.body_html:
Echinodorus Red Phoenix is a variety of Amazon Sword native to South America. With nicknames like Red Phoenix Sword, it is a popular sword plant thanks to its rich color palette, consisting of deep green to reddish tones. Like other Echinodorus species, this aquarium plant is extremely resilient, making it a solid pick for novice aquascapers or aquarium hobbyists just getting into planted tanks.

To bring out more ruby tones, inject CO2, intensify lighting and supplement with quality aquatic plant fertilizer. However, this is all optional! Amazon Sword plants are well-loved for their adaptability and are also brilliant in Wabi Kusa style setups and low tech tanks. In these environments, the leaves tend to be firmer with a primarily green color palette. Overall, this is a versatile choice for any aquascape and friendly to all skill levels.

Common Name Amazon Sword
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Background
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Moderate
Height 5-15"+

products.title: Giant Duckweed (Spirodela Polyrhiza)
products.body_html:
Spirodela polyrhiza is a larger form of the notorious, fast-growing floating plant, Duckweed. Giant Duckweed features bright green, rounded leaves that float freely on the surface of planted aquariums or ponds, producing a serene, lily-like look.

It is on equal footing with classic Duckweed and provides the same water-purifying power, but is a little easier to manage and thin out as needed. Alongside natural water filtration, floating plants are great for providing shade and shelter to aquarium fish or shrimp and indirectly help with algae control. It is relatively hardy, making it a favorite aquatic plant for beginner aquarium hobbyists. Overall, adding Giant Duckweed to your aquarium or pond is a great, low-maintenance way to grow beautiful and functional floating plants!

What to do with excess Giant Duckweed

This pond and aquarium floater grows quickly, so it’s important to monitor its growth and remove excess as needed to prevent shading of other aquatic plants that need light to grow. When disposing, please do not release it into local waterways, ponds, or down your plumbing. Try composting it or allowing it to dry out completely before discarding in the bin. It can also be incorporated into soil mixtures for house plants or gardens! Thank you for being a responsible aquarium hobbyist and helping protect native ecosystems!

Common Name Giant Duckweed / Greater Duckweed
Care Easy
Lighting Moderate 
CO2 Not Required
Suggested Placement Aquarium Floater
Growth Rate Fast

products.body_html:

A Glass Aqua favorite! Ryuoh Stone is a beautiful hardscape material that offers unique characteristics and is typically available in a variety of sizes and shapes. Used primarily for Iwagumi style planted aquarium layouts, Ryuoh Stone creates clean lines and its rich charcoal grey coloration with white striations pair well with green and red hues provided by aquarium plant species. Characteristics of this popular hardscape stone include white marbling, unique grooves and jagged edges. We also recommend using Ryuoh Stone as a solid foundation in nature style aquarium layouts utilizing aquarium driftwood. Ryuoh Stone is dense and can be considered medium-weight. In perspective, Ryuoh is lighter than dense aquarium stones such as Hakkai Stone and not as porous as Black Lava Stone. A solid, well-rounded aquarium stone hardscape choice!

products.title: Selaginella Kraussiana Aurea
products.body_html:
Selaginella Kraussiana 'Aurea' is a variety of club moss well-known for its delicate, lacy foliage. Although these plants resemble ferns, they are not true ferns or mosses! They fall somewhere in between and have several nicknames like peacock fern, spike moss and more. Selaginella species make wonderful terrarium plants and are commonly used for ground coverage or as trailing vines in planted setups.

Like other Selaginella species, this cultivar appreciates elevated humidity and well-draining substrate. It is also suitable for attaching to natural driftwood or porous structures, where it will slowly attach and spread. Avoid placing it too close to or exposed under direct, high light, as this can burn the plant. If you're looking for a low-maintenance terrarium plant that provides good coverage with beautiful texture, this is a great choice! Its growth can be random, so occasional pruning is recommended to maintain a neat appearance. Trimmings can be easily relocated or attached elsewhere to propagate the plant.

Common Name Selaginella Kraussiana
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Moderate (Filtered)
CO2 Terrariums
Placement Ground cover / Trailing
Propagation Trim and replant
Growth Rate Moderate
Size Varies

products.title: Pacific Driftwood Stump
products.body_html:
Pacific Driftwood is a natural wood commonly used for aquascaping, planted aquariums and terrariums. With textures and colors similar to Malaysian Wood, these stump pieces bring plenty of character and detail to the foundation of your planted tank. They’re ideal for base-layering and are often slightly flatter to support your statement driftwood pieces. Like all natural driftwood, it will likely float, so dedicate some time to ensure it's thoroughly soaked. However, Pacific Wood is denser than many other types, reducing soak times. Overall, this natural wood is a solid, versatile layout material that remains a team favorite.

products.title: Aquario Neo C Plants Water Conditioner

- Removes chlorine and heavy metals like Neo C
- Supports beneficial bacteria colonization like Neo Plant Booster

products.title: Hygger Sponge Filter
products.body_html:
Aquarium sponge filters are a classic form of filtration that have long been trusted in the aquarium hobby. They’re easy to clean and offer a no-fuss, reliable approach to aquarium filtration. The Hygger Sponge Filter features a dual-chamber: the sponge provides mechanical filtration, while the lower chamber is filled with bio-ceramic media that supports beneficial bacteria to help break down organic waste.

Sponge filters work by using an air pump to pull water through the sponge that then traps particles that would otherwise contaminate the water. This tried-and-true method removes waste, keeps water clean, and helps prevent ammonia spikes that are dangerous for fish and shrimp. The integrated air stone creates the classic “bubbling” for water movement and essential oxygenation. This type of filtration is especially popular for shrimp tanks and similar setups that need gentle waters. Each filter includes an extra sponge, detachable container, ceramic bio-media, and is available in three sizes for tanks ranging from 5 to 125 gallons. (Air pump and tubing purchased separately)

Sponge Filter Highlights
- Combines mechanical and biological filtration for efficient waste removal and cultivation of beneficial bacteria.
- Tried and true! Sponge filters are a safe filter for shrimp tanks and similar setups.
- Easy to take apart for routine cleaning. (We like to squeeze our sponges in the water removed during tank maintenance!)

products.title: Drosera Spatulata 'Labill'
products.body_html:
Drosera spatulata, commonly known as a the Spoon-Leaved Sundew, is one of the largest groups of sundews found in nature. Native to many regions around the world, this carnivorous plant is highly adaptable and a favorite among collectors. It’s often recognized for its self-seeding behavior, sometimes becoming a “weed” in Drosera collections, but this trait also makes it easy to grow and share with friends or family.

This sundew forms cute clusters of rounded leaves covered in glimmering, sticky tentacles that attract and trap tiny insects. Like other carnivorous plants, it grows best in nutrient-poor soils and obtains nutrition primarily from prey. A balanced mix of sphagnum moss with perlite or sand provides the ideal blend of aeration and moisture retention. Along with mostly moist conditions, Drosera prefer water that is low in minerals and salts like rain or RO water. These plants add beautiful color and texture to terrariums and appreciate bright light that isn’t too direct. Easy to grow and quick to flower, this sundew is a wonderful beginner-friendly introduction to carnivorous plants!

Common Name Spoon-leaved Sundew
Care Easy
Lighting Moderate to High
CO2 Non-aquatic
Terrarium Placement Bright & waterlogged
Propagation Leaf or root cuttings
Growth Rate Varies
Size Varies

products.body_html:

Under bright light, its spoon-shaped leaves and sticky tentacles develop a scarlet hue that is sure to stand out in a terrarium or in any carnivorous plant collection. Like other Drosera, it captures small insects and is a fascinating plant to grow and observe.

It grows best in consistently moist, bog-like conditions. If you decide to grow it on its own, you can keep its pot in a shallow dish of water. Alternatively, if you choose to add it to a planted setup, pick a spot where it has good access to water at its base. Drosera plants will do best with distilled, rain or RO water sources.

products.body_html:
Bucephalandra is often regarded as a beginner-friendly aquarium plant for aquascaping, though it’s not chosen as frequently as other popular types like Anubias, Amazon Sword, or Java Fern. However, this family of aquatic aroid plants shouldn't be overlooked! Commonly called Buce, this flowering genus brings dynamic color, texture, shapes, and sizes to any planted tank. Freya is a variety we're not yet familiar with, but we like what we see. It features rounded, ruffled leaves with a distinct rib running down the center and bold green color that’s nearly black. Under the right aquarium light, it’s sure to produce beautiful contrast and the signature shimmer Bucephalandra are known for.

Bucephalandra are a wonderful choice for everything from nano aquariums to large aquascapes and even terrarium enclosures. They’re highly adaptable and forgiving plants, with the rewards often outweighing any risks. When planted in nature aquariums, keep the rhizome above the substrate and attach it to driftwood, rocks, or any porous surface. products.title: Selaginella Pulvinata
products.body_html:
Selaginella pulvinata is a lovely evergreen perennial, commonly treated like terrarium mosses. This variety of club moss or spike moss forms dense, vibrant green tufts that grow in a rosette-like pattern. Depending on the age of the plant, it may also develop a "trunk", giving it a miniature tree appearance. In natural settings, S. pulvinata can be found growing on limestone rocks at high elevations, making it relatively hardy and resistant to drier conditions once mature.

One of its unique traits is its response to changing environments. When dry, its fronds curl inwards, almost as if to preserve energy, and once moistened, they fan out and redevelop a rich green hue. Keep it in a growing medium like sphagnum moss or peat moss, and avoid waterlogging. Mist it daily and allow excess water to run off its leaves and into the medium. While it grows slower than other well-known Selaginella species, this moss-like plant is great for anyone looking to add an uncommong addition to terrariums, vivariums, or planted enclosures.

Common Name Club Moss / Spike Moss
Care Easy

CO2 Recommended
Terrarium Placement On driftwood, rocks or decor
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Slow
Size Varies

products.title: Willow Moss
products.body_html:
Willow Moss (Fontinalis antipyretica) is a freshwater aquarium moss with intricate, delicate fronds and a cascading growth pattern. Although it grows slowly, it does so steadily, and with proper care, it will eventually form lush, fluffy mats of green in aquascapes of all sizes. Like other aquatic mosses, Willow Moss is exceptionally easy to propagate—any small piece that breaks off from the main mass will attach to a new surface and produce new growth. In aquascaping, live moss is commonly used in a variety of popular planted aquarium styles. It is ideal for softening hardscape edges and adding layered, textured dimension. Willow Moss is suitable for nano aquariums, low-tech tanks, and even terrariums where environments are consistently moist.

Aquarium mosses are popular for their resilient nature and adaptability, tolerating a wide range of water conditions with minimal care requirements. This makes Willow Moss a top pick for beginner hobbyists. Use it in your nature aquarium, nano planted tank, low-light aquarium, betta tank, or shrimp tank. No matter what type of setup you have, Willow Moss is sure to add that extra bit of green your tank needs and aquatic life loves!

products.body_html:
Water Poppy (Hydrocleys nymphoides) is a beautiful aquatic plant known for its glossy, lily-like leaves and bright green color. In warmer conditions, it blooms cute yellow flowers that resemble poppies, hence its common trade name. If you're looking for pond plants that add character and texture to an outdoor or indoor pond, or a large planted aquarium with a marginal area, this one’s a great pick!

Water poppies provide several benefits, including natural filtration and coverage for fish or shrimp. They are generally easy to grow when planted in nutritious soil and provided with high light. In outdoor setups, this aquatic plant loves full sun but does not tolerate colder climates well. When happy, regular trimming is necessary to manage its growth and spread, and it can be propagated easily by separating runners and replanting.

Common Name Water Poppy
Care Easy
Lighting High
CO2 Optional
Aquascape Placement Background
Propagation Separate runners
Growth Rate Moderate
Size 10+”

products.title: Microsorum Pteropus 'Windelov' (Hybrid)
products.body_html:
Java Fern 'Windelov' Hybrid (Microsorum Pteropus) is a rich green aquatic plant that combines characteristics from popular species. While the exact parentage is unclear, this unique hybrid features Windelov’s classic split tips on overall thicker leaves. This aquatic fern also appears smaller in size, making it a nice choice for nano planted aquariums or aquascapes that need small plants to fill open spaces in a layout.

If Windelov Hybrid is like any other Java Fern, it is likely an easy aquatic plant that can add greenery and life to low-tech aquariums or setups with low lighting. Java Fern plants are known for their adaptability, making them a top recommended plant for beginners. Attach it to stones, driftwood, or aquarium decor and watch this naturally bushy plant grow!

Common Name Java Fern
Care Easy
Lighting Low
CO2 Optional
Aquascape Placement Midground
Propagation Separate and attach
Growth Rate Slow to Moderate
Size Varies

products.title: Build Your Own Aquarium - Shrimp Tank
products.body_html:
Build Your Own Shrimp Tank

Looking to start your own freshwater shrimp tank? Whether it’s your first time or you’re a seasoned hobbyist setting up another, you’re in the right place! Curate your next setup in one place with our BYOB kit. Start by choosing your rimless tank, then add essentials like substrate, accessories, aquatic plants, and of course, the stars of the show—shrimp! These tiny critters are fun to watch and will graze on biofilm, algae, and debris all day. Peaceful and lively, they add interest and life to any nature aquarium.

products.title: Crystal Red Shrimp
products.body_html:
Crystal Red Shrimp are one of the most popular dwarf shrimp varieties in the aquarium hobby. These freshwater aquarium shrimp are easy to spot thanks to their bold color and unique pattern. In a planted aquarium, they pop beautifully against live aquatic plants and are super fun to observe. They are not recommended for beginners and are considered more challenging to keep. Due to their specific water requirements and natural sensitivity to environmental shifts, crystal reds usually have dedicated setups. When successful, nurturing a colony is a highly rewarding experience!

These shrimp are considered soft water shrimp and need slightly acidic conditions, extremely low TDS levels, and stable temperatures between 68–75°F. Provide gentle flow and ensure regular water changes to keep the tank clean. CRS will happily graze and forage, but also appreciate a varied diet whenever possible.

Tank size recommendation: 5-10G

Shrimp are extremely sensitive to copper. If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your invertebrates.
CRS shrimp are peaceful. Keep them in an aquarium with non-agressive tank mates to ensure their safety.

products.title: Aquarium Filter Tube Hose Cleaning Brush
products.body_html: An essential item for the modern aquascaper, planted aquarium enthusiast or aquarium keeper! This simple aquarium filter tube hose cleaning brush is ideal for cleaning the interior of aquarium filter tubing and glass aquarium lily pipes. With time, everyday gunk, organic matter, and buildup collect inside filter tubing requiring periodic maintenance to ensure a clean appearance and optimal flow for the entire planted aquarium tank. Made from durable materials, this aquarium filter tube hose cleaning brush is intended for long term use and its double-ended brush options allow various size hoses, tubes and more to be thoroughly cleansed.

products.title: Yellow Goldenback Shrimp
products.body_html:
Yellow Goldenback Shrimp (Neocaridina davidi) are a lovely dwarf shrimp you'll commonly see in freshwater planted tanks. Known for their bright yellow color, they stand out beautifully against aquatic plants and darker substrates. In peaceful aquariums, these critters spend most of the day foraging for biofilm and algae, making them both a lively and practical addition.

These shrimp are peaceful and hardy in nature, making them great for beginner and experienced hobbyists alike. In safe, healthy conditions, aquarium shrimp breed fairly easily. When setting up a shrimp tank, make sure to provide plenty of shelter and hiding spots. Choose non-aggressive tank mates, and keep in mind that some fish may view shrimplets as food. Gentle filtration is best, and if you’re using a canister filter, consider an intake cover to protect young shrimp from being sucked in.

Tank size recommendation: 5-10G

Shrimp are extremely sensitive to copper. If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your invertebrates.
Yellow Goldenbacks are peaceful. Keep them in an aquarium with non-agressive tank mates to ensure their safety.

products.body_html:

Customize your next reef aquarium with our BYOB aquarium kit. Start by selecting your tank, then piece together the main components of a successful reef tank! Add essential equipment like hardscape, lighting, and optional macroalgae for design, refugium, or both. products.body_html:

Bring your aquascaping vision to life with our BYOB hardscape builder! Mix and match aquarium stones, natural driftwood, and accent pieces to create the foundation for your planted aquarium. Whether you’re creating a classic nature-style scape, a minimalist planted tank, or a nano aquarium where detail work shines, our bundle builder makes it easy to customize your hardscape all in one swoop.

Hardscape sets the stage for your planted tank and can influence everything from aquatic plant choice to livestock. Build it your way and create an aquascape layout that’s uniquely yours!

products.title: Bucephalandra Pearl Grey
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Pearl Grey is a lovely Bucephalandra plant that originates from Indonesia. This easy aquarium plant features broad, rounded leaves that display deep emerald tones with a silvery sheen when grown under aquarium lights. Like others in the family, this Buce is often considered beginner-friendly. It is hardy in most conditions and takes some effort to cause serious damage to the plant.

Thanks to its resilience, it can grow happily in a wide variety of planted setups. Whether you have a low tech planted aquarium or a terrarium filled with rare aroids, this plant is a sure fit. To grow it, attach it to a porous surface like driftwood or rock and place as desired. In planted aquaria, we recommend choosing a spot with good flow and supplementing with CO2 and quality fertilizer. Once mature, Bucephalandra Pearl Grey can be propagated by separating the rhizome and reattaching. A great low-effort, high-reward aquatic plant!

Common Name Bucephalandra / Buce
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Recommended
Suggested Placement Attach to wood or stone
Propagation Separate rhizome
Growth Rate Slow
Leaf Size Varies

When used with a pH reagent solution, the solution changes colors to indicate whether there is an excessive, optimal, or insufficient amount of CO2 being injected into the planted aquarium. Using a CO2 drop checker offers an extremely simple method for monitoring CO2 levels in the planted tank. It's a great help for those who are new to CO2 injection, a good reminder to refill your CO2 cylinder and fantastic for any aquarist that is keeping aquatic plants with specific requirements. products.title: Blue Dream Shrimp
products.body_html:
Blue Dream Shrimp bring life and beautiful blue color to any planted aquarium. Selectively bred, this Neocaridina shrimp originates from Taiwan and is a popular pick for dwarf aquarium shrimp keepers! They stay relatively small, making them a great choice for nano aquariums or peaceful community tanks. We especially like watching them graze in tanks that have plenty of green aquatic plants. They spend the majority of their day grazing on algae and biofilm that's commonly found on plants or hardscape.

Like other Neocaridinas, they are adaptable but can be sensitive to sudden changes. We recommend adding them to aquariums that are fully cycled and stable. They prefer tropical temperatures and slightly soft water. As with any freshwater setup, a good filter is key for maintaining water quality. Exclusive shrimp keepers enjoy using sponge filters, but if you opt for a canister, consider getting an intake sponge or guard to prevent shrimplets from getting sucked up into the filter.

Tank size recommendation: 5-10G

Shrimp are extremely sensitive to copper. If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your invertebrates.
Blue Dreams are peaceful. Keep them in an aquarium with non-agressive tank mates to ensure their safety.

products.title: Black King Kong Shrimp
products.body_html:
Black King Kong first appeared in the planted aquarium hobby in the early 2000's. These freshwater aquarium shrimp feature black, white, and hints of blue hues, making them a high-contrast and beautiful addition to any planted tank. Their patterns can vary—some fully solid, others color blocked or slightly speckled. One of the classic Taiwan Bees, they're also commonly cross-bred with other popular species to create hybrids and unique color patterns.

BKK shrimp are a little more sensitive, but still considered easy to care for. Basic care includes using remineralized RO water, stable temperatures, and pH levels between 6–6.5, along with quality nutrition. Many shrimp keepers prefer sponge filters for their gentle operation and aeration. Otherwise, these little critters make a great addition to any peaceful freshwater setup. They spend much of their time grazing and eating algae or biofilm from aquarium plants, glass, substrate, and hardscape.

Tank size recommendation: 5-10G

Shrimp are extremely sensitive to copper. If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your invertebrates.
Black King Kong shrimp are peaceful. Keep them in an aquarium with non-agressive tank mates to ensure their safety.

products.title: Green Jade Shrimp
products.body_html:
Green Jade Shrimp feature, as their name suggests, a mix of green tones that can carry hints of yellow, blue, or black depending on the individual shrimplet. They’re a unique addition to any planted aquarium and will happily forage throughout the tank all day. While their algae consumption isn’t as strong as Amano Shrimp, they breed easily, and large numbers grazing together can help clean obscure areas that are otherwise hard to reach.

Like other Neocaridina shrimp, they are adaptable but can be sensitive to sudden changes. We recommend adding them to established setups. They prefer tropical temperatures and slightly soft water. As with any planted aquarium, a good filter is essential for maintaining water quality. Depending on what type of filter you have, consider getting an intake sponge or guard to prevent shrimplets from getting sucked up into the filter. (We've found entire colonies living in ours!)

Tank size recommendation: 5-10G

Shrimp are extremely sensitive to copper. If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your invertebrates.
Green Jades are peaceful. Keep them in an aquarium with non-agressive tank mates to ensure their safety.

products.title: Aponogeton Henkelianus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton Henkelianus is another bulb plant that is similar to Madagascariensis in appearance. However, this variety can be distinguished by solid green color throughout and narrower leaves. Not as readily available, this aquarium bulb adds beautiful texture to planted tanks when healthy. A solid pick for any aquascaper who loves unique aquatic plants.

Important Note: This listing includes a bulb that may or may not be sprouted at the time of purchase. Please ensure you are prepared to care for either option.

Provide a nutrient-rich substrate or supplement with root tabs to support this plant’s development. While most are relatively hardy, their leaves are fragile and should be handled with care to prevent tears. Start with moderate lighting, good water flow and CO2 injection if possible. Patience will be rewarded with this one!

Common Name Madagascar Lace
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Background
Growth Rate Moderate to Fast
Height 8-20+"

products.title: Echinodorus Big Bear
products.body_html:
A moderately sized Amazon sword plant for planted tanks and aquascaping! Echinodorus ‘Big Bear’ features a mixed color palette ranging from emerald green to crimson tones. Its colors change depending on the environment in which it is grown, making it an intriguing aquarium plant to cultivate. When grown above water, its leaves are rounded, more rigid, and primarily green. When grown submerged in aquarium conditions, the leaves soften, move with the flow, and develop deep burgundy tones.

Like others in the family, this is considered an easy aquatic plant. It works well for low tech tanks or low light aquariums and will be resilient in most conditions. Sword plants are heavy root feeders and will benefit from nutritious aquasoil and root tab supplementation. To boost growth and form, provide moderate lighting and CO2 injection. Overall, Big Bear is easy going and is a great option for all aquascaping skill levels. We also like this one as a main plant for Wabi Kusa style setups!

Common Name Amazon Sword
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Background
Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Moderate
Height 15-20"+

products.title: Echinodorus Major Martii
products.body_html:
Echinodorus Major (Martii), commonly known as Ruffled Amazon Sword, is a bright green easy aquarium plant that is great for large freshwater aquariums and aquascaping. This sword plant grows broad, ruffled leaves that grow in an outward pattern like a fountain. Its impressive size makes it suitable for large setups or as a focal point in planted tanks.

This amazon sword plant is popular for its easy care and large size. Like other Echinodorus, it is a heavy root feeder and will benefit from nutritious aquasoil and root tabs. Moderate lighting and CO2 injection will boost growing speed and form! Overall, this is an easy aquatic plant that's great for hobbyists of all skill levels.

Common Name Ruffled Amazon Sword
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional

products.title: Crinum Thaianum
products.body_html:
Crinum Thaianum is a unique aquarium bulb plant that belongs to the lily family but closely resembles something else we all know. Its bulb looks like an onion—earning its common trade name, Thai Onion or Water Onion. When mature, this aquatic plant develops long, ribbon-like leaves that can grow very large if allowed. Due to its potential size, it’s a great option for anyone with a large planted tank or sizable pond where it can fully flourish!

Important Note: This listing may or may not include a fully mature plant. Please ensure you are prepared to care for bulb plants at all growing stages.

Aside from space requirements, Crinum Thaianum is an easy aquarium plant to grow and keep. It’s undemanding, and the reward outweighs the risk in this case. Established, mature plants will occasionally bloom fragrant lilies that rise to the water’s surface and last for some time. A great live plant for enthusiasts who enjoy keeping aquariums or similar setups long-term.

Common Name Thai Onion / Water Onion
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Background
Growth Rate Moderate to Fast
Height 8-20+"

products.body_html:
Build Your Own Aquarium Conditioner Bundle

Keeping a freshwater aquarium healthy starts with the water itself. Our BYOB aquarium conditioner bundle lets you curate a custom set to keep your tank stable and healthy for your fish, shrimp and aquatic plants. Quality liquids and treatments are key for maintaining water quality, supporting healthy plant growth, supporting livestock health and keeping a well-rounded aquarium that’s in good health.

Check out the essential stages of care, reduce stress for your livestock, support plant growth and prevent common water-quality related problems before they start. Whether you’re starting a new tank or maintaining an established setup, customize a regiment that works for you and your aquarium.

products.title: Saxifraga Stolonifera (Strawberry Begonia)
products.body_html:
Saxifraga Stolonifera is an ornamental (and very cute!) plant. Commonly called Strawberry Begonia, it’s not actually a strawberry plant or a begonia, but gets its name from its physical traits and growth habit. Native to Asia, it is sometimes miscategorized as a geranium, though it belongs to the Saxifraga family, which also includes Jade Plants. In the right conditions, Strawberry Begonias will put out trailing stolons and baby plantlets. Its fuzzy leaves display a veined pattern with undersides that range from red to purplish hues.

It's important to highlight that this is not an aquarium plant, but it would work beautifully in an open terrarium-like setup. It tolerates most household humidity levels and appreciates moderate to bright, filtered lighting. Intense direct sunlight or grow lights may scorch the plant and cause more harm than good. If you choose to pot it traditionally, ensure your mix drains well, avoid wetting the leaves, and prevent standing water. Otherwise, fertilize every two weeks or once a month. It is fairly easy going and will reward you with baby plants to propagate or share with friends and family.

Common Name(s) Strawberry Begonia
Care Easy to Moderate
Lighting Medium to High [Filtered]
Growth Rate Slow to Moderate
Size Varies Depending on Conditions

products.title: Egeria Najas
products.body_html:
Egeria Najas, often called Anacharis or Elodea Najas, is a fast-growing aquarium stem plant popular for many freshwater aquarium applications. It can be planted in the background of an aquascape to create a full, feathery backdrop, or used as a floating plant in shrimp tanks and breeding projects where it provides both shelter and filtration. Comparable to Egeria Densa, this species has finer, more delicate foliage with lightly serrated edges, adding soft texture and a natural look to planted aquariums.

Easy to care for, Egeria Najas adapts well to a wide range of water parameters, making it suitable for beginners and hobbyists who enjoy live plants without extra effort. Its quick growth and nutrient uptake support water quality while contributing to a healthy, balanced aquarium. Please check your state’s regulations and protect the environment by disposing of excess trimmings or plant matter into the trash. Do not release this plant into drains or introduce it into local waterways.

Common Name Anacharis / Waterweed
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Midground to Background
Propagation Trim and plant stems
Growth Rate Fast

products.title: Aponogeton Ulvaceus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton Ulvaceus is a large aquarium bulb plant originating from Madagascar. Its slight translucent, twisted rich green leaves create a beautiful focal point in planted tanks. Capable of growing up to 2ft, Aponogeton plants are best for large planted aquariums, ponds or setups that provide enough space for its height and expansive spread.

Important Note: This listing does not include fully mature plants. Please ensure you are prepared to care for bulb plants that may or may not be sprouted at the time of purchase.

Otherwise, as long as your tank is big enough, Ulvaceus is relatively easy to care for, hardy and will adapt to a range of water conditions. To speed up growth, adding CO2 will produce a significant difference. Like other fish tank bulbs, it may periodically enter periods of dormancy. During this time, its leaves may die off, but don't mistake it for plant death! Allow it some time to rest and it will resprout once it enters a growing cycle again. If you're looking for a fast-growing plant that adds lovely swaying motions and enrichment for your fish or shrimp, give Aponogeton Ulvaceus a go!

Common Name Aponogeton Ulvaceus
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional

products.title: Schismatoglottis Sp. Mamasa
products.body_html:
Schismatoglottis sp. Mamasa is a rare tropical aroid that originates from regions in Southeast Asia. A member of the same family as Anubias, Bucephalandra, Philodendron, and more, this aroid plant offers uniquely textured leaves that are deep green in color. There isn’t much information known about this specific variation, its care is likely similar to other popular terrestrial and semi-aquatic plants in the same family or genus.

These types of plants are generally hardy and will do well when planted in a well-draining medium, such as a standard aroid mix or even sphagnum moss. Provide a quality plant fertilizer every so often, along with low to moderate indirect light to prevent leaf scorching. We imagine this plant would work beautifully in terrarium-like enclosures or paludarium setups where water sources are nearby, humidity is high, but the plant itself is not submerged.

Common Name Schismatoglottis Roseospatha

products.title: Anubias Nana Peacock
products.body_html:
Anubias Nana 'Peacock' is likely a cross-bred hybrid between classic Nana and another similar variation from the genus. This aquarium plant features deep green color and lightly rounded leaves with pointed tips. Like other plants, it's safe to assume that sp. Peacock is easy to care for and can be treated the same as other hardy Anubias plants. If you're looking for an easy care aquatic plant for your fish tank or terrarium, give this one a try.

Basic care requirements for Anubias include low to moderate light and a liquid plant fertilizer or source of nutrients. Because it’s an epiphyte, it can be attached to natural driftwood or aquarium rocks. Don’t bury the rhizome, and make sure it has good flow to get started. This family of aquatic plants is slow-growing and can develop algae on leaves if placed in low flow or under too much light. Learn to balance the key elements in a planted aquarium and you’re golden!

When finished, just pop it off the tubing, let it dry completely, and store it away until next time. Works great for planted aquariums, freshwater fish tanks, barebottom tanks and more.

Regular water changes are one of the most important parts of the aquarium hobby. Keeping the water clean and clear from organic buildup and toxins ensure better health for your fish, shrimp, snails, aquarium plants and more. This gravel vac attachment creates a gentle flow that won’t disturb your aquascape and helps prevent fish or shrimp from being sucked up during maintenance. Other than that, this aquatic plant grows at a moderate rate. When happy, it spreads via runners through the substrate and will provide good horizontal coverage in planted tanks. In lower light settings, it would also make a great transitional plant from the midground area to the background section of the tank.

products.title: Anubias Nana Tropica
products.body_html:
Anubias Barteri var. Nana ‘Tropica’ is a hardy aquatic plant. Its moderate size is still suitable for nano aquariums, but can also be a great addition to larger planted tanks. It features more compact growth due to shorter stems, and broad, dark green leaves. Like other Anubias, it is easy to care for, making it a great choice for beginner aquarists. Anubias leaves are also on the thicker side, making them better choices in fish tanks that contain herbivores that may graze or snack on live aquarium plants.

An aquatic rhizome, it can be attached to natural driftwood or stones. Its ability to grow without being planted makes it versatile with placement and an awesome choice for filling in empty spaces throughout an aquascape layout. Support faster growth and form with CO2 injection and a liquid plant fertilizer! Alternatively, if you’re searching for a good terrarium plant, consider giving this one a try. Its broad leaves are a perfect green filler and its hardiness is tough to beat.

products.title: Aponogeton Longiplumulosus
products.body_html:
Aponogeton longiplumulosus is an aquatic bulb plant that originates from Madagascar. It offers a bright olive-green color and large, fluted foliage that adds a delicate look to any planted tank. This aquarium plant grows large and tall, so we recommend it for larger tanks or even as a pond plant! When happy, it grows fast and adds lovely swaying motion and plenty of coverage for tropical fish and shrimp.

This is an easy aquatic plant that adapts well to most water conditions. As a bulb plant, it will appreciate a nutritious aquasoil to support healthy growth. To plant aquarium bulbs, bury them partially into your substrate of choice and make sure the sprout tip remains above the surface. If you’re using sand, please ensure you have nutrient root tabs on hand. Be aware that some Aponogeton species go through dormant periods when new growth may slow or stop completely. This doesn’t mean your plant is dead—give it some time (typically a few weeks), and it should reboot if planted in the right conditions.

Common Name Aponogeton Longiplumulosus

Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional

products.title: Duckweed (Lemna Minor)
products.body_html:
Duckweed Lemna Minor

Lemna Minor, commonly known as Duckweed, is a fast-growing, resilient and low-maintenance floating aquatic plant. Its petite, bright green leaves add a delicate botanical look to any planted aquarium or pond. As its name implies, Duckweed is a top floating aquarium plant pick that is well-known for its rapid growth. This makes it a great addition to aquariums or ponds with a high bioload. Through its tiny root system, Duckweed happily absorbs excess nutrients and is as a great source of natural water filtration. Beyond supporting improved water quality, aquarium floaters also create a layer of coverage and extra security for a variety of aquarium and pond fish. Many fish species, such as fancy goldfish, will also enjoy snacking on Duckweed!

For a beautiful, nature-style look in your planted tank or pond, try mixing duckweed with other floating plants like Pistia stratiotes (water lettuce) or Phyllanthus fluitans (red root floaters).

What to do with excess Duckweed

Duckweed grows quickly, so it’s important to harvest and thin it regularly to prevent overgrowth and shading of submerged plants that need light to grow. When disposing of excess duckweed, please avoid releasing it into local waterways, ponds, or down your plumbing. We recommend composting it or allowing it to dry out completely before discarding. It can also be incorporated into soil mixtures! Thank you for helping protect native ecosystems.

Common Name Lemna Minor (Duckweed)
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Moderate 
CO2 Not Required
Suggested Placement Aquarium Floater
Growth Rate Fast

products.title: Marimo Moss Ball
products.body_html:
Marimo Moss Balls (Aegagropila linnaei / Cladophora aegagropila) aren’t actually moss! They’re a rare, slow-growing algae that naturally forms into soft, velvety green spheres. In the wild, Marimo grows at the bottom of lakes in Japan, where water currents tumble the algae into its signature moss ball shape. The name Marimo directly translates to “seaweed ball” in Japanese, and it’s arguably the only algae species welcomed openly in freshwater aquariums.

Marimo Balls are very much alive and make a unique, moss-like addition to any freshwater aquarium or planted tank. They’re often used in low light tanks for greening, an aquascaping technique where moss is attached to driftwood and rocks to create an aged, natural look. Simply pull the ball apart and gently wedge into cracks or attach with a very small amount of adhesive. Marimo Balls are also decorative on their own and are often kept in glass jars or similar containers. They’re great for betta fish tanks, shrimp setups, and more. If your Marimo floats, it likely has air trapped inside. Squeeze it gently to release and drop it back in!

Quick and easy examples of the greening method and how to unball a Marimo Moss Ball:

How to care for a Marimo Ball

Marimo Balls require very little maintenance. They prefer cooler water temperatures below 76°F. In still water or under stronger lighting, you may need to manually rotate them occasionally to ensure all sides receive light. Otherwise, they’re fine in low light, tolerate a wide range of water parameters, and can even survive in slightly brackish water. Their hardy nature and minimal care makes them a top beginner “aquarium plant”!

Common Name Marimo Ball
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Optional
Suggested Placement Loose / Attached to hardscape
Propagation Gently separate
Growth Rate Varies
Height Varies

products.title: Plant Pile - Iwagumi Aquarium Plant Bundle
products.body_html:
Aquascaping and planted tanks go hand in hand, with many styles that are popular for different reasons. Iwagumi layouts hold a special place with our team. This plant pack includes carpeting plants ideal for an Iwagumi aquarium, pre-bundled to suit different rimless tank sizes.

What aquatic plants are best for Iwagumi aquascapes?

Traditional Iwagumi planted tanks typically feature one to three plant species. Carpet plants like Micranthemum 'Monte Carlo', Hemianthus Callitrichoides (HC), Glossostigma Elatinoides, and hair grass varieties are the most common choices. All of these plants grow low and compact in a horizontal manner. The goal is to complement the hardscape layout, not overshadow.

How many plants do I need to grow an aquarium carpet?

It depends — everyone’s preferences, layout style, and budget are a little different. To make it easier, we’ve put together safe, bundled aquarium plant packs sized for common tank dimensions to help you get started. Check the recommendations for your tank below, and if you’re unsure or have questions, feel free to email our team — we’re happy to help as best we can.

Size Tank Volume Plants Included Tank Recommendations
NANO 2 - 5G [5] UNS 5N Nano Rimless Tank
MED 10 - 15G [10] UNS 45U Standard Rimless Tank
LRG 18 - 30G [20] UNS 60U Standard Rimless Tank

What is Iwagumi?

It’s an art form that is inspired by ancient Japanese and Chinese rock garden formations that replicate natural landmarks. This style of aquascaping focuses on meticulous placement of rocks, followed by a minimal selection of aquatic plants. Plants chosen should compliment the rock layout, not outshine. If you’re interested in learning more about the basics of Iwagumi aquascaping, we have a full in-depth blog article here.

products.title: Black Miyuki Medaka Rice Fish
products.body_html:
Oryzias latipes "Black Medaka"

Commonly called Japanese Ricefish or Japanese Killifish, Medaka are peaceful freshwater fish that are often enjoyed from a top-down view and have been bred into many color morphs. They are not often seen in planted aquariums and are more popular for indoor/outdoor ponds, patio tubs, and other top-view planted setups. They're hardy in nature, have low bioloads and can tolerate a wider range of water parameters so long as they're stable. Though they're a popular pond fish, Japanese Ricefish have calm temperaments and can definitely be a great addition to community tanks.

Rice fish do best in groups, and generally occupy mid to upper levels of aquariums and ponds. They're active swimmers and easy to care for, making them a great choice for beginners as well. If being considered for a planted tank, provide gentle flow and live aquatic plants for coverage and reduced stress. Japanese Rice Fish are omnivorous and will readily accept quality flake, pellet, live, and frozen foods — and, as an added bonus, they will eat any mosquito larvae they find, making them both lovely and useful for outdoor planted tanks or ponds!

Tank size recommendation: 10G per school of 6 Rice Fish
If using tap, be sure to use a dechlorinator to ensure safety for your livestock.

products.title: Eriocaulon Vietnam
products.body_html:
Eriocaulon sp. ‘Vietnam’ is a less common aquatic plant, known for its bright green, grass-like leaves. It resembles Eriocaulon sp. ‘Goias’ but can be distinguished by its size and slightly arched leaves. Although this genus has a reputation for being difficult to grow, this variety is relatively forgiving and often considered one of the easier Eriocaulon species. Still, it requires the basics: quality aquasoil, good lighting, CO₂ injection, and a complete nutrient source with both micro- and macronutrients.

When healthy, it forms dense tufts that resemble grass, making it an excellent midground choice in a planted tank. Successfully growing this plant rewards you with a unique and beautiful addition to your aquascape. If you’re looking for a reliable plant to grow at the base of stones, Eriocaulon are solid contenders!

Common Name Eriocaulon Vietnam
Care Advanced
Lighting High
CO2 Required
Suggested Placement Midground
Propagation Separate and replant

products.title: Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Jwala'
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Jwala'
This aquarium plant is a variation of Cryptocoryne Spiralis! Truthfully, information about this cultivar is still limited within the planted aquarium community. Based on the original species, it likely produces slender, elongated leaves in varying shades of green, brown, red, and olive. Like most Cryptocoryne plants, we believe it would work well in the midground area, where it will add texture and a good transition between different areas of an aquascape layout. Plus, Crypts look great at the base of stones and dritwood.

This family of aquarium plants is known for being low-maintenance and beginner-friendly. However, this comes with caveats! Cryptocoryne should be planted in mature, cycled aquariums that are well-balanced between light and nutrient levels. Use quality aquasoil and allow them to send down roots before attempting to relocate or propagate to reduce chances of crypt melt!

products.title: Persicaria Hydropiper
products.body_html:
Persicaria hydropiper, also known as knotweed or marsh waterpepper, is a marginal stem plant with slender, lance-shaped green leaves and an upright, bamboo-like growth habit. Its delicate texture and growing form creates a subtle calming effect and planted aesthetic that many aquascapers can appreciate. It will soften emerged sections of an aquascape and can develop pinkish to purple hues under strong light and in nutrient-rich substrate. In nature, it grows along wetland edges, making it a must-have for ponds, paludariums, and hybrid planted tanks with emersed sections.

If you're searching for a unique plant that can be grown multiple ways, try it out! Persicaria Hydropiper can be used in nature-style planted tanks or as an accent plant for your Wabi Kusa. It offers rich color and relatively easy care, making it a great choice for various aquascaping styles. Provide it with moderate to strong lighting, nutrients and CO2 injection if planting in your aquarium.

products.body_html:
Myriophyllum elatinoides ‘Snowflake’ is a beautiful aquarium plant that features fine, feather-like leaves that fan out in patterns similar to snowflakes! Its form and rich green color bring natural texture and softness, making it a great choice for planted tanks and aquascapers who enjoy mixing and matching different aquarium stem plants. A fast-growing stem plant, Myrio plants are great for the midground or background of planted aquariums, where they have plenty of room to grow tall and sway with the flow. If you have a pond, the top-down view of this Myrio plant is special, and we highly recommend giving it a try.

General care for Myriophyllum stem plants includes moderate to high light, CO₂ injection, and nutrient-rich aquasoil. When happy, you’ll need to trim it regularly. Replant healthy tops, and soon enough, you’ll have dense, feathery bushes in your planted tank or pond—your rice fish, tropical school, or shrimp will love it too. Overall, this aquarium plant is a solid pick for aquascaping, especially nature-style planted tanks, indoor ponds, or Dutch-style aquascape layouts!

products.title: Myriophyllum sp. 'Roraima'
products.body_html:
Myriophyllum sp. ‘Roraima’ is a large, colorful aquatic stem plant. This special milfoil is best suited for bigger planted aquariums, where its needle-like leaves and intense colors can really shine in the background of an aquascape. The ‘Roraima Milfoil’ is a cultivar within the Myriophyllum genus that’s considered one of the easier types to care for—and it doesn’t fall short in the looks department. When grown in a healthy aquarium, its feathery whorls develop a vivid color palette that includes orange, yellow and deep red hues. It is a fast-growing aquatic stem plant that branches naturally and responds well to routine pruning. To get the best growth, provide this red aquarium stem plant with strong lighting, CO2 injection, and a well-rounded dosing regimen that includes both micro and macronutrients.

With patience and consistent care, Myriophyllum sp. ‘Roraima’ brings color, texture and height to any aquascape. It’s a great fit for nature-style planted tanks, dutch-style aquascapes or large-scale freshwater ponds.

products.body_html:
SL Aqua MORE Nature Aquarium Soil is a nutrient-rich, natural black aquasoil. The soil is sintered—a process where it is heated at a high temperature to create a strong bond, resulting in granules that are durable and won’t break down easily, yet remain porous enough to allow water and nutrients to pass through. It contains organic acids and essential nutrients that naturally lower pH and KH, creating ideal conditions for many live aquatic plants and tropical fish. MORE Nature aquasoil is easy to work with and can be used for many popular aquascaping styles. Use it to build sloped substrate hills for Iwagumi aquascapes or as a base layer for a colorful nature-style planted tank.

What's the difference between normal and powder aquarium soil?
Available in powder and medium granule sizes, its different grain options accommodate many popular plants. Fine powder substrate is great for small-rooted aquatic plants like Helianthus callitrichoides ‘Cuba’ (HC) or Eleocharis parvula ‘Mini’ (Dwarf Hair Grass), while the medium size works well for Cryptocoryne, Echinodorus, Rotala, Ludwigia, and more.

How much substrate do I need?
The general rule of thumb is one 3L bag per 5 gallons and one 8L bag per 10 gallons

[Tank Length x Tank Depth x Desired Substrate Height (inches)] / 60 = Total liters of Aquarium Soil required

The Ultum Nature Systems Peninsula Reef System offers a reef-ready foundation for your next saltwater aquarium. Designed for performance and modern aesthetic, the system is available in three sizes and two stand colors. All systems feature ultra-clear glass tanks, black silicone and signature 45° mitered edges. The included aluminum stand arrives fully assembled with a built-in sump and pre-glued plumbing for quick and convenient setup. Place the tank, connect the pipes using twist-on unions, and you’re ready to go.

Its strong magnetic doors are fully removable, providing wide access for equipment organization, wire management and routine tasks. A built-in storage drawer, dedicated chiller space and integrated controller box with surge protection offer a clean, organized solution for essential reef gear. On the drawer side, you’ll notice three purposefully placed holes that make it easy to run cords neatly across the entire length of the system—especially helpful if your power outlet is on the opposite end, preventing messy, dangling wires. Included controller boards are removable and accessible. Durable polycarbonate lids are resistant to warping and help reduce salt creep.

The peninsula system’s overflow box features a slim profile with intake slots at the top and bottom, plus a built-in glass guard to help prevent flooding in the event of pump failure. For water control, gate valves ensure smooth operation and reduced noise, while a ball valve and barbed fittings on the return line make external accessory connection simple. A gasket beneath the drain pipe and sump connection further reduces salt creep build-up.

Inside the multi-chamber sump, a prefilter baffle captures large debris, extending the life of filter socks, while diffuser plates muffle sound and keep them in place. The second chamber is designed to fit various protein skimmers and the third chamber offers flexibility for additional media or use as a refugium, all with adjustable water levels. Finally, an acrylic ATO with a pre-installed float valve makes automatic top-offs seamless. Ultum Nature Systems Reef Systems include a variety of features and can be customized to fit your tank’s needs—ensuring performance and long-term health for your reef aquarium.

products.title: Rotala Rotundifolia Shimoga
products.body_html:
Rotala Rotundifolia 'Shimoga' is an aquarium stem plant that stands out! This variation features slightly translucent, soft green leaves that curl upward to reveal baby pink undersides. Its a medium-sized Rotala that grows upright. This quality makes it a great background plant for standard to large planted aquariums. With its distinctive leaf shape and palette, Rotala Shimoga is a great aquarium plant that provides texture and pops of color to any aquascape.

For the best form and color, plant it in a tank it with CO2, moderate to high lighting and a nutrient-rich aquasoil. Once established, try adding root tabs or liquid aquatic plant fertilizers for essential micro and macronutrients. You'll know your Rotala is happy when it starts growing fast and needs regular trimming. On the bright side, you can replant cuttings to achieve healthy bushes, start another planted tank or share!

Common Name Rotala sp. 'Shimoga'
Care Moderate
Lighting Medium to High
CO2 Recommended
Suggested Placement Background
Propagation Trim and plant stems
Growth Rate Fast
