products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Purple
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra are low maintenance aquatic plants that are similar to Anubias and other aquarium rhizomes. They often feature varying hues that change depending on light fixtures and viewing angle. This quality makes them a highly-coveted species amongst aquarists. They're ideal for planted tanks of all sizes and can also be planted in terrariums or high-humidity planted setups. Attach them to your favorite driftwood or stone and place in your tank as desired.

Like other species, buce brownie purple offers easy care and can be added to low light tanks or high tech tanks. If planted in a low tech without CO2, keep the lighting low and ensure consistent maintenance is performed to prevent algae growth on leaves. For quicker growth and better color, CO2 injection, moderate light exposure and a quality liquid aquariumn plant fertilizer will produce noticeable differences.

Common Name Bucephalandra / Buce
Care Easy
Lighting Low to Medium
CO2 Recommended
Suggested Placement Attach to wood or stone
Propagation Separate rhizome

products.title: Bucephalandra Artemis
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra, or Buce for short, can get overlooked when thinking about the best beginner plants—but these beautiful aquatic plants are easy to care for just like Anubias or Java Fern! They’re also generous with looks and often feature iridescent, speckled leaves that shimmers in a freshwater planted aquarium. With a wide range of species available to the aquarium community, Buce is a solid choice for everything from a nano tank to standard sized aquariums.

Artemis is no different. It features subtle color that shines under aquarium LEDS. Its slightly waved leaves will add movement to your tank while giving small tropical livestock a place to rest or hide. Like other species, it is an easy care plant and can be planted in low light tanks with little fuss. If you want quicker results, add your buce to a high tech setup with CO2, moderate lighting and nutrients.

products.title: Dipteracanthus Elastica
products.body_html:
Dipteracanthus Elastica is a low-profile terrestrial plant that's fantastic for all-purpose coverage in terrariums and similar planted setups. It may be easier to remember this one by some of its common names like bubble plant or popcorn plant! It earns these nicknames thanks to its small, round, puckered leaves. The bubble plant also features a naturally dense growth structure, providing an instantly lush look in exchange for minimal effort. Like most terrarium plants, Diptercanthus Elastica will appreciate good humidity levels and low to moderate lighting. Avoid placing it under strong direct lights to prevent burning it!

If you're searching for an oddball plant for your terrarium, container garden or paludarium, try adding a Bubble Plant. Its rich green color, paired with its fun texture, is sure to add visual interest—and bonus! When the plant is happy, it may even bloom violet-blue flowers.

products.title: Persicaria Praetermissa
products.body_html:
Persicaria Praetermissa, misidentified as Polygonum sp. 'Ruby', is a water knotweed that can be found growing in marginal conditions in tropical areas of Asia and Australia. This plant typically grows along the edges of wetlands, but can be cultivated under water. When grown in an aquarium, it takes on an entirely different form. Its leaves take on a slender shape with pointed tips and displays a mix of brownish-red and orange hues.

If you're searching for a unique plant that can be grown multiple ways, try it out! Persicaria Praetermissa can be used in nature-style planted tanks or as an accent plant for your Wabi Kusa or emersed portion of your paludarium. It offers rich color and relatively easy care, making it a great choice for various aquascaping styles. Provide it with moderate to strong lighting, nutrients and CO2 injection if planting in your aquarium.

products.title: Sea Lettuce Ulva Macroalgae
products.body_html:
Live portion of Sea Lettuce macroalgae. For best practice, always quarantine any new arrivals, regardless of the source, to ensure the health of your tank.

Ulva Lactuca is a nutritious and easy to grow macroalgae for saltwater aquariums. Better known as Sea Lettuce, it is recognized by its bright green color and delicate structure that is semi-translucent throughout. Macroalgae use in marine tanks is commonly associated with nutrient export in refugiums, thanks to popular types like Chaeto or Dragon’s Breath. However, this super seaweed offers a different benefit: It is fast growing and nutritious.

These qualities make it a solid, natural food to have on hand that most saltwater invertebrates and fish will happily snack on. It is also relatively hardy so long as it’s provided with strong lighting and moderate flow. When feeding, it’s best to offer it directly or use a device to keep it contained. The same goes for growing Sea Lettuce. Separate holding tanks or buckets are recommended since it can spread easily and root in the main aquarium display.

Common Name Sea Lettuce / Ulva Seaweed

products.title: Cryptocoryne Thwaitesii Kirindoya Brown
products.body_html:
Cryptocoryne Thwaitesii 'Kirindoya Brown' is a slow-growing aquatic plant that features textured, olive-brown tones and slightly ruffled leaves. Like other species in the same family, Cryptocoryne plants are fairly undemanding and don't require special care to grow properly. They are most commonly used as a midground aquarium plant in aquascapes due to their moderate size and low profile growth.

When given the correct care, it will form dense tufts of foliage that will add a lovely touch to any planted aquarium. With all plants, we recommend using a quality aquarium LED light, CO2 injection and nutrient dense aquasoil to yield the best results. If your soil is low in nutrients, consider adding an all-purpose liquid fertilizer to support improved color and form. To propagate, simply cut off new plantlets growing from the mother rhizome.

Anubias Barteri is a wonderful aquarium rhizome plant that is commonly used in low light tanks. The cultivar 'Round Leaf' features chubby, spade-shaped leaves with noticeable veins throughout. Its ability to grow in low tech aquariums and general hardiness makes it popular amongst beginner hobbyists. It is very forgiving of many water conditions and still looks great without extensive or complicated care. Most Anubias are evergreen and provides rich color to freshwater planted tanks or terrariums.

products.title: Eriocaulon Aquaticum
products.body_html:
Eriocaulon plants have become more prevalent in the planted tank hobby. With different plant hobbies becoming closer, many aquarists have started integrating terrestrial and marginal plant species into hybrid tanks. Eriocaulon Aquaticum lands into that section perfectly! In natural settings, this plant can be found growing along the water's edge of bogs and wetlands, making it adaptable and suitable for both submerged and emersed growth. Its rosette form offers structure and easy maintenance given its non-chaotic growth. So, if your planted tank or paludarium is in need of a grass-like plant that provides texture but won't take over like regular hair grass varieties, Eriocaulon might be the move.

However, we'll throw caution to beginners! Like other species in the family, Eriocaulon Aquaticum is recommended for more experienced aquarium hobbyists. These plants need specific care that isn't difficult, but requires good understanding of water parameters, nutrition and lighting. If your Eriocaulon plant is growing happy, it will have white, firm roots and consistent uniform growth. Overall, Eriocaulon provide beautiful texture and neat, tufted growth that is loved by many aquarists.

Common Name Eriocaulon / Erio
Care Advanced
Lighting High
CO2 Required

Propagation Separate and replant
Growth Rate Moderate

When happy and healthy, Bucephalandra easily becomes a focal point in a planted tank and it becomes easy to understand why so many aquarium hobbyists love these plants. products.body_html:
Rotala sp. 'Myanmar'

Rotala is a diverse genus of aquarium stem plants that are loved for their vivid coloration, fast growth, and versatility in plant related hobbies. These plants feature delicate leaves that come in a myriad of colors including bright green, orange, red and pink tones. A classic name in the planted tank hobby, there is a Rotala for every aquascape.

The most commonly known types like Rotala Rotundifolia and Indica, feature many cultivars within their respective groups. These aquatic stem plants are fairly adaptable, but grow best in planted tanks with CO2 injection, moderate to high lighting and nutrient-rich waters. When conditions are optimal, consistent maintenance is required to maintain a manicured look or to achieve the coveted bushy appearance. Once established, Rotala can be easily propagated by trimming its stems and replanting. Most species can also grow in emersed conditions, making them suitable for Wabi Kusa setups and terrariums!

While it adapts well to low-tech aquariums without CO2, its growth form will be noticeably different with thinner, greener leaves.

With a moderate growth rate, Samolus parviflorus ‘Red’ requires periodic trimming but is much easier to manage than faster-growing stem plants. Its forgiving nature, unique appearance, and soft color make it a great choice for aquascapers looking to add red tones without the extra care demands of more sensitive species.

products.title: Dr. Tank Iron Sticks
products.body_html:
Dr. Tank Iron Sticks aim to boost and improve aquatic plant health. Iron (Fe) plays a key role in facilitating the production of chlorophyll. All plants use this to photosynthesize. By supporting this imporant process, aquarium plants are able to better absorb light and use it as needed to grow fuller and healthier. Better health results in enhanced colors and resilience to stress. Providing aquarium plants with quality supplements goes a long way and plays a key role in overall tank balance and health. Iron sticks may be a good choice if your planted tank is using a sand only substrate or you've yet to introduct a liquid plant fertilizer.

Dr. Tank aquarium care products work best when used in tandem with consistent maintenance. Iron supplementation alone will not automatically boost or enrich plant coloration.

- Easy to follow instructions included.
- Supports planted aquarium health and can be used in heavily or sparsely planted setups.
- Handmade from natural ingredients.

Cryptocoryne Spiralis 'Tiger' is a unique aquarium plant that features long ribbon-like leaves with a striped pattern that earns its name. Cryptocoryne aquarium plants have a repuation for being easy care plants—this is true when the plant is mature and has developed a strong, healthy root system. To mitigate effects, we recommend planting Cryptocoryne Spiralis Tiger (and other crypt plants) in an established, cycled aquarium when possible. Supplement this aquarium plant with moderate lighting and quality aquarium soil or aquatic plant root tabs if using sand / gravel. Additionally, CO2 injection and aquarium plant fertilizer will benefit all aquarium plants long term. When established and growing well, increasing lighting levels will help boost color! products.title: Koi Galaxy Male Betta Fish
products.body_html:
Male Galaxy Koi Betta Splendens (Betta Fish)

Galaxy Koi Bettas have the marble gene variant and are known for their color-changing patterns. This gives each betta a unique personal color palette and marbled appearance that evolves over time, making each individual fish one-of-a-kind. The Koi Galaxy Betta often features a Halfmoon Plakat (HMPK) tail type, offering several advantages such as improved swimming ability and reduced fin nipping. Overall, Betta Splendens are a top fish pick for many aquarium hobbyists! Keep reading to learn more about Bettas.

Sex: Male
Type: Halfmoon Plakat (HMPK) Galaxy Koi Male
Size: 2-3"
Color/Pattern: Breeder's choice - May include shades of blue, red, orange, yellow, purple and more.

Quick Introduction to Betta Fish
Betta Splendens, commonly known as Betta fish or Siamese fighting fish, belong to a genus that includes over 70 recognized species. Popular for beginners, Bettas are often picked as a first pet fish thanks to their vivid color, interactive behavior and easy care. They are also great for those with limited space, as they don’t require large aquariums to thrive. While Bettas are generally hardy, this also makes them commonly mistreated. To help your Betta live a healthy and happy life, we’ve put together essential Betta fish care tips and tricks!

How to care for Betta Fish
The first step in proper Betta fish care is maintaining good water conditions. These fun fish prefer slightly acidic water around 6.8, but will tolerate a range between 6-8. In the wild, Bettas live in waters that are rich in organic matter that contribute to this preference. To emulate similar conditions, consider using materials in your aquarium tank that will naturally help lower pH. We like using natural driftwood or Indian Almond Leaves (IAL) to help gently lower pH levels. In addition, maintaining a clean and well-kept aquarium is essential. Always keep a dechlorinator to ensure chlorine and heavy metals are removed during routine water changes and tank maintenance.

What type of filter does a Betta need?
Quality aquarium filtration is key for all freshwater fish, and should never be overlooked. Modern Betta varieties are often bred to have large fins, which contribute to poor swimming and sensitivity to strong currents. Avoid aquarium filters that produce strong direct flow. Instead, opt for filters or outflow attachments that diffuse water to provide your betta with gentle flow.

Ideal water temperature for Betta fish
Betta fish originate from warm, tropical regions where temperatures range between 76°F and 80°F. Water that is too cold or too hot can lead to poor health, sluggish behavior and a weakened immune system.

Bonus Betta Care Tips
- Add live aquarium plants! Betta fish love to forage, explore and will greatly appreciate some coverage. Live aquatic plants also help with water oxygenation and purification.
- Daily exercise. Use a reflective surface to encourage a good flare. Consistency helps your Betta stay strong and active.
- Use an aquarium lid. Bettas are great jumpers, so a tank lid is recommended. Avoid sealed fits as Bettas need to come to the surface for air.

Want to learn more about Betta fish care? Check out our blog for in-depth guides
Part 1 - Tank setup, water preparation, aquarium cycling, filtration, heating, live plants and decor.
Part 2 - Tank maintenance, Betta food, flare exercises, recommended tank mates, natural supplements, remedies, illnesses, diseases, medication and short overview.

Eriocaulon Buergerianum

Eriocaulon species have become more popular and common in the planted aquarium hobby. Eriocaulon Buergerianum, like other popular Erio plants, grows best when provided with soft water, a nutrient rich aquasoil, high lighting and elevated CO2 levels. While some aquarium plants are known for obtaining nutrients through the water column, Eriocaulon absorb through their root systems, making the use of a quality aquarium substrate or aquatic plant fertilizer tab necessary for better chances of successful growth. Proper growth for Eriocaulon presents as white and firm roots along with rate of new growth and overall uniformity. Eriocaulon species tend to grow in an organized manner and greying roots with lopsided growth may indicate poor health.

Once familiar with Eriocaulon care and growth, these aquarium plants are moderate growers and can be split carefully. This family of aquatic plants are a solid choice for midground placement in mature aquascapes. Eriocaulon provide excellent texture and compact, tufted growth that is loved by many aquarists.

products.title: Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost
products.body_html:
Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost is a lovely buce specimen that is characterized by its iridescent quality and deep brownish red and purple-blue tones that shimmer under aquarium LEDs. This gem-like aquarium plant is an epiphyte, like all Bucephalandra, and features compact growth with soft wavy leaves. When grown in clusters, Bucephalandra provide visual interest, delicate texture and also serve as coverage for smaller aquarium critters like freshwater shrimp and fry. These aquarium plants grow best when attached to natural materials like driftwood or aquarium stones.

While Bucephalandra species are adaptable and hardy, they thrive best in stable, mature aquariums. Bucephalandra tolerates most water parameters, but a slightly lower pH is preferred. This can be achieved by using pH-lowering aquarium liquids, indian almond leaves (IAL) or adding natural driftwood to the tank. Bucephalandra Brownie Ghost and other varieties of Buce benefit from steady water temperatures, CO2 injection and good flow. When choosing where to plant Buce, avoid dead zones in the aquarium as poor circulation combined with excess nutrients may result in algae growth on its leaves. Propagation is simple and can be done by gently separating the rhizome and reattaching as desired.

products.title: Aciotis Acuminifolia
products.body_html:
Aciotis Acuminifolia

Aciotis Acuminifolia is a rare and uncommon stem plant that can be grown in aquarium and terrarium conditions. It made its debut to the aquarium hobby in the early 2000's, but is still not commonly seen in aquascaping or similar hobbies today. This unique stem plant exhibits a strict vertical growth pattern with foliage that features prominent venation. This aquarium plant's color palette offers a mixture of green, red, orange and yellow tones that will vary and shift depending on the aquatic plant's growing conditions. When kept submerged in a planted tank, Aciotis Acuminifolia will develop vivid red undersides of its leaves. New growth presents lighter in color with upturned leaves that slowly fan out and flatten as the aquarium plant matures. Aciotis Acuminifolia is an aquatic stem plant that we recommend for planted aquarium hobbyists that have decent experience growing aquarium plants that fall within an intermediate to advanced level. Aciotis Acuminifolia is a moderately sized aquarium plant and will grow anywhere between 3-15 inches in height with a 2-3" spread, making it a good choice for a range of popular aquarium and terrarium tank sizes.

Aciotis Acuminifolia requires soft water conditions, moderate to high lighting, CO2 injection and a quality aquatic plant fertilizer that offers both essential micro and macronutrients. If successful, Aciotis Acuminifolia can be propagated similarly to other aquarium stem plants by trimming along the stem and replanting in nutritious aquarium substrate. If preferred, Aciotis Acuminifolia is suitable for terrarium conditions too! Its emersed growth prompts the display of bold red colors, serrated leaf edges and pink flowers.

Common Name Aciotis Acuminifolia

Like other popular aquarium driftwoods, manzanita driftwood will initially float and should be soaked to ensure the pieces are waterlogged. products.title: Bacopa Monnieri Platinum
products.body_html:
Bacopa Monnieri Platinum A beautiful silvery-green aquarium plant for aquascaping and planted aquariums!

Bacopa Monnieri 'Platinum' is a rare mutation of the popular aquarium plant Bacopa Monnieri. This new variation of Bacopa produces variegated growth with a range of green, cream and silver hues. Depending on tank conditions, Bacopa Platinum can provide a dense growth pattern and is a great choice for aquarium hobbyists in search of a unique midground or background aquatic plant.

Suitable for most hobbyists, Bacopa Monnieri Platinum grows well with moderate to high lighting to support its variegated leaves. Bacopa plants are typically tolerant of many aquarium conditions and are resilient for most freshwater aquariums. However, for best results, we recommend providing this aquarium plant with nutrient-dense aquarium substrate, CO2 injection and a quality aquatic plant fertilizer. To achieve a bushy look, regular pruning of Bacopa Monnieri Platinum is necessary to encourage more compact growth, off-shoots and a fuller group of plants. Propagation can be done by trimming along its stem and replanting into the substrate.

products.title: Chaetomorpha Macroalgae
products.body_html:
Live, golfball-sized chaetomorpha macroalgae for saltwater refugium. This is a live, aquatic organism. May come with pods. For best practice, always quarantine any new arrivals, regardless of the source, to ensure the health of your tank.

Chaetomorpha, commonly referred to as Chaeto, is a popular choice of macroalgae for reef tank refugiums. Its fibrous structure effectively absorbs excess nutrients, such as nitrates and phosphates, helping to maintain optimal water quality. Chaeto's fast growth and low-maintenance care make it a favorite among reef hobbyists looking for a simple, natural solution to nutrient management without the need for complex equipment. It also provides shelter for beneficial organisms like copepods and is visually appealing, with some hobbyists even choosing to keep a portion in the main display tank, as it is usually left untouched by most marine life.

In summary, Chaeto is easy to grow and maintain with proper lighting, offering a straightforward way to support a balanced and healthy marine aquarium.

Common Name Chaeto
Chaetomorpha
Chaetomorpha Linum
Spaghetti Algae
Care Easy
Lighting Moderate to High

Temperature 72-80°F

products.title: Red Ogo Gracilaria Macroalgae
products.body_html:
Live, golfball portion of Red Ogo (Gracilaria Pacifica) macroalgae for saltwater refugium. This is a live, aquatic organism. For best practice, always quarantine any new arrivals, regardless of the source, to ensure the health of your tank.

This variety of Red Ogo, commonly known as Red Spaghetti Ogo, gets its name from its resemblance to other ogo seaweed variations. True to its name, Red Spaghetti Ogo (Gracilaria Pacifica) features a deep red-brown hue and a stringy growth pattern. Its texture allows it to form dense mats that are capable of absorbing excess nutrients, supporting a more balanced ecosystem. Relatively easygoing, Red Ogo isn't difficult to grow and will require intermittent harvesting. Harvested Gracilaria will be readily accepted by herbivores in the main aquarium.

Overall, Red Ogo Spaghetti Macroalgae is an excellent choice for saltwater refugiums, as it efficiently consumes excess nitrates and phosphates. It also adds unique texture to dedicated macroalgae aquariums. However, keep in mind that if the aquarium contains herbivores, this macroalgae is likely to be consumed!

Red Spaghetti Ogo offers a simple and effective way to maintain a balanced and healthy marine aquarium.

products.title: Dragon's Breath Macroalgae
products.body_html:
Live, 1-1.5" portion of dragon's breath (Halymenia Durvillei) macroalgae for saltwater refugium. This is a live, aquatic organism. For best practice, always quarantine any new arrivals, regardless of the source, to ensure the health of your tank.

Dragon's Breath Macro Algae (Halymenia) is a popular macroalgae, known for its vibrant red and purple hues and branchy, ribbon-like structure. Its tips often fork, giving it the distinct shape and texture that make it a favorite among reefers. Like other macroalgae, Dragon's Breath provides reliable nutrient export, making it a solid choice for the refugium, but is also equally popular as a decorative feature in main reef displays and dedicated macroalgae aquariums. For optimal growth, Dragon’s Breath thrives in moderate to high flow and, when used in a refugium, benefits from a tumbling motion under strong lighting. If being used in an ornametal way, it should be noted that herbivorous and omnivorous marine life may graze and consume it! A beautiful pop of color for your reef aquarium and a tasty snack. Keep some extra growing in your refugium, just in case!

In summary, Dragon's Breath is easy to grow and requires less maintenance when compared to other faster growing macro algae varieties. It provides a straightforward way to support a balanced and healthy marine aquarium.

products.title: UNS Bonsai 021
products.body_html:
The Ultum Nature Systems new line of Bonsai Tree driftwood features hand-crafted pieces that are carefully made by skilled artisans. The UNS Bonsai 001 features a waved style, perfect for creating sweeping aquascape layouts. Aquascaping with Bonsai Trees has become popular and offers numerous benefits to the planted aquarium. Each tree features distinctive features and can be embellished with a variety of popular aquatic plant species such as Anubias, Bucephalandra, Java Fern, Aquarium Moss and more. The addition of bonsai tree driftwood to the aquarium also softens water, provides shelter and a source of food for smaller aquarium critters like caridina shrimp and snails.  

Almost all driftwood is buoyant. Before use, we recommend allowing your UNS Bonsai Tree to thoroughly soak to ensure the tree is fully waterlogged. If this step is not preferred, simply attach a weight to the bonsai tree to ensure it is weighed down when the aquarium is filled. Aquarium driftwoods release tannins and will discolor aquarium water, giving it a tea colored tint. This is temporary and will reduce gradually over time as water changes are performed. Tannins are 100% natural, providing several benefits to the aquarium such as softening the water and emulating natural conditions of many tropical fish. If you do not like colored water, adding this product to your filter system will leave you with crystal clear water quickly!

What we like about Ultum Nature Systems Natural Bonsai Driftwood Trees

Hand-crafted: Each UNS Bonsai Tree Driftwood piece is handcrafted by a skilled artisan from natural wood.

products.body_html:
This listing includes a mature, mother plant specimen. Echinodorus plants are a wonderful choice for beginner aquarists.
Remove this plant from its growing medium before planting.

Echinodorus Red Rubin is a beautiful hybrid of Echinodorus Horemanii Red and Echinodorus Barthii. This aquarium plant offers bold coloration and is suitable as a focal point or centerpiece aquarium plant in larger planted aquarium tanks. Echinodorus Red Rubin also exhibits horizontal growth if the lighting is strong enough and can grow quite wide. This should be kept in mind when deciding on placement.

New growth displays a green tone and later matures into deep, bold red hues provided that optimal conditions and care are met. This aquatic sword plant is on the larger spectrum of the Echinodorus family and is suitable for planted aquarium tanks with ample space for it to spread its leaves. Like all aquatic sword species, Echinodorus Red Rubin is easy to keep, fairly hardy and needs a minimum of a nutrient-rich aquarium substrate as these species of aquatic plants are heavy root feeders. Aside from this, Echinodorus species are fairly tolerable of varying water parameters and lighting conditions. Given that this aquatic sword grows fairly large and robust, pruning periodically will be required to ensure smaller aquatic plants are not shaded from the light provided.

products.body_html:

Microsorum Pteropus, commonly known as Java Fern, is a classic aquarium plant in the planted tank hobby. A fantastic choice for beginner aquarists, Java Fern has earned a reputation for being nearly bulletproof as it almost requires some effort to kill and will tolerate a wide range of aquarium conditions. Microsorum Pteropus is an epiphytic plant making it versatile with placement. This aquarium plant will happily grow attached to aquarium driftwood or when placed in the crevices of an aquascape layout. Its bright green, broad foliage is a great way to fill in areas lacking coverage. Like most aquarium plant species, Java Fern can be grown in low tech conditions, but the addition of CO2 injection will produce a noticeable difference in growth rate and appearance. 

Microsorum Pteropus can be attached directly to driftwood, rocks, or any aquarium decoration using thread or glue. Make sure to keep the rhizomes above your substrate or the plant can begin to rot. Propagation is easy and straightforward; simply cut or pull apart rhizomes to be replanted/reattached.

Check your system carefully—if the holes are missing, you'll need a specialized drill bit to create them before installation.



Designed for saltwater aquariums, the Ultum Nature Systems Mini Refugium Light is waterproof, submersible, and easy to customize based on your tank's needs. The included inline remote provides precise control over brightness levels and includes a built-in timer with a memory function for easy automation. With 8 color modes and 10 dimmable levels, users can customize lighting to support optimal Chaetomorpha or other macro algae growth and improved filtration. Refugiums are an optional but beneficial addition to saltwater aquariums as they improve filtration and promote biodiversity by providing a safe, protected habitat where copepods, amphipods, and other beneficial microfauna can thrive.

products.title: Floating Moss Ball
products.body_html:
Vesicularia Montagnei, more commonly known as Christmas Moss or X-mas Moss is arguably one of the most popular aquatic mosses commonly used in the planted aquarium hobby. Ideal for beginners, this low-maintenance moss adapts to a wide range of parameters and requires minimal care. This listing includes Christmas Moss pre-attached to a lightweight sphere attached to clear fishing line and small weight, producing a floating appearance. Offering an ornamental look, these floating moss balls are a great addition to low light aquariums and are a great gift to beginner hobbyists or for anyone who enjoys whimsical aquarium decor.

Aquarium Moss is a top choice for all skill levels. Once established, Christmas Moss is a quick growing moss and may require regular pruning to ensure a manicured look. Manual removal is best, but if trimming is required, we recommend turning off filters and pumps to prevent the moss spreading to unwanted areas.

products.title: Dr. Tank Trace Nutrient Sticks
products.body_html:
Dr. Tank Nutrient Sticks support healthy aquatic plant growth for your planted aquascape. Made from the same ingredients found in nutrient-dense aqua soil, Dr. Tank Trace Sticks provide a source of trace elements to support healthy aquarium plant plant growth. To use, insert one Trace Nutrient Stick one inch deep for every 6x6" area of planted substrate with aquarium plants. With a slow-release formula, Trace Sticks provide ongoing benefits for 60-90 days, ensuring long-term aquarium health and healthier aquatic plants.

Dr. Tank aquarium care products work best when used alongside regular maintenance. Excessive levels of nutrients in the aquarium can lead to algae blooms and other issues.

Product Highlights

- Easy to Use. Each bottle contains 15 sticks. Use according to instructions as needed.
- Contains balanced levels of trace elements. Trace elements contribute to both plant coloration and growth form. While high levels are not required, the presence of trace elements makes a noticeable difference and should be provided regularly.
